     // child units make sense.\n574:                 let _ = child.unit()?;\n575:                 CalcUnits::empty()\n576:             },\n577:             CalcNode::Anchor(..) | CalcNode::AnchorSize(..) => CalcUnits::LENGTH_PERCENTAGE,\n578:         })\n579:     }\n580: \n581:     /// Negate the node inline.  If the node is distributive, it is replaced by the result,\n582:     /// otherwise the node is wrapped in a [`Negate`] node.\n583:     pub fn negate(&mut self) {\n584:         /// Node(params) -> Negate(Node(params))\n585:         fn wrap_self_in_negate<L: CalcNodeLeaf>(s: &mut CalcNode<L>) {\n586:             let result = mem::replace(s, CalcNode::dummy());\n587:             *s = CalcNode::Negate(Box::new(result));\n588:         }\n589: \n590:         match *self {\n591:             CalcNode::Leaf(ref mut leaf) => {\n592:                 if leaf.map(std::ops::Neg::neg).is_err() {\n593:                     wrap_self_in_negate(self)\n594:                 }\n595:             },\n596:             CalcNode::Negate(ref mut value) => {\n597:                 // Don't negate the value here.  Replace `self` with it's child.\n598:                 let result = mem::replace(value.as_mut(), Self::dummy());\n599:                 *self = result;\n600:             },\n601:             CalcNode::Invert(_) => {\n602:                 // -(1 / -10) == -(-0.1) == 0.1\n603:                 wrap_self_in_negate(self)\n604:             },\n605:             CalcNode::Sum(ref mut children) => {\n606:                 for child in children.iter_mut() {\n607:                     child.negate();\n608:                 }\n609:             },\n610:             CalcNode::Product(_) => {\n611:                 // -(2 * 3 / 4) == -(1.5)\n612:                 wrap_self_in_negate(self);\n613:             },\n614:             CalcNode::MinMax(ref mut children, ref mut op) => {\n615:                 for child in children.iter_mut() {\n616:                     child.negate();\n617:                 }\n618: \n619:                 // Negating min-max means the operation is swapped.\n620:                 *op = match *op {\n621:                     MinMaxOp::Min => MinMaxOp::Max,\n622:                     MinMaxOp::Max => MinMaxOp::Min,\n623:                 };\n624:             },\n625:             CalcNode::Clamp {\n626:                 ref mut min,\n627:                 ref mut center,\n628:                 ref mut max,\n629:             } => {\n630:                 if min.lte(max, PositivePercentageBasis::Unknown) {\n631:                     min.negate();\n632:                     center.negate();\n633:                     max.negate();\n634: \n635:                     mem::swap(min, max);\n636:                 } else {\n637:                     wrap_self_in_negate(self);\n638:                 }\n639:             },\n640:             CalcNode::Round {\n641:                 ref mut strategy,\n642:                 ref mut value,\n643:                 ref mut step,\n644:             } => {\n645:                 match *strategy {\n646:                     RoundingStrategy::Nearest => {\n647:                         // Nearest is tricky because we'd have to swap the\n648:                         // behavior at the half-way point from using the upper\n649:                         // to lower bound.\n650:                         // Simpler to just wrap self in a negate node.\n651:                         wrap_self_in_negate(self);\n652:                         return;\n653:                     },\n654:                     RoundingStrategy::Up => *strategy = RoundingStrategy::Down,\n655:                     RoundingStrategy::Down => *strategy = RoundingStrategy::Up,\n656:                     RoundingStrategy::ToZero => (),\n657:                 }\n658:                 value.negate();\n659:                 step.negate();\n660:             },\n661:             CalcNode::ModRem {\n662:                 ref mut dividend,\n663:                 ref mut divisor,\n664:                 ..\n665:             } => {\n666:                 dividend.negate();\n667:                 divisor.negate();\n668:             },\n669:             CalcNode::Hypot(ref mut children) => {\n670:                 for child in children.iter_mut() {\n671:                     child.negate();\n672:                 }\n673:             },\n674:             CalcNode::Abs(_) => {\n675:                 wrap_self_in_negate(self);\n676:             },\n677:             CalcNode::Sign(ref mut child) => {\n678:                 child.negate();\n679:             },\n680:             CalcNode::Anchor(_) | CalcNode::AnchorSize(_) => {\n681:                 wrap_self_in_negate(self);\n682:             },\n683:         }\n684:     }\n685: \n686:     fn sort_key(&self) -> SortKey {\n687:         match *self {\n688:             Self::Leaf(ref l) => l.sort_key(),\n689:             Self::Anchor(..) | Self::AnchorSize(..) => SortKey::Px,\n690:             _ => SortKey::Other,\n691:         }\n692:     }\n693: \n694:     /// Returns the leaf if we can (if simplification has allowed it).\n695:     pub fn as_leaf(&self) -> Option<&L> {\n696:         match *self {\n697:             Self::Leaf(ref l) => Some(l),\n698:             _ => None,\n699:         }\n700:     }\n701: An app unit is 1/60th of a CSS device pixel, which is, in turn\n24:  * an integer number of device pixels, such at the CSS DPI is as close to\n25:  * 96dpi as possible.\n26:  */\n27: \n28: using nscoord = int32_t;\n29: inline constexpr nscoord nscoord_MAX = (1 << 30) - 1;\n30: inline constexpr nscoord nscoord_MIN = -nscoord_MAX;\n31: \n32: namespace mozilla {\n33: struct AppUnit {};\n34: \n35: // Declare AppUnit as a coordinate system tag.\n36: template <>\n37: struct IsPixel<AppUnit> : std::true_type {};\n38: \n39: namespace detail {\n40: template <typename Rep>\n41: struct AuCoordImpl : public gfx::IntCoordTyped<AppUnit, Rep> {\n42:   using Super = gfx::IntCoordTyped<AppUnit, Rep>;\n43: \n44:   constexpr AuCoordImpl() : Super() {}\n45:   constexpr MOZ_IMPLICIT AuCoordImpl(Rep aValue) : Super(aValue) {}\n46:   constexpr MOZ_IMPLICIT AuCoordImpl(Super aValue) : Super(aValue) {}\n47: \n48:   template <typename F>\n49:   static AuCoordImpl FromRound(F aValue) {\n50:     // Note: aValue is *not* rounding to nearest integer if it is negative. See\n51:     // https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=410748#c14\n52:     return AuCoordImpl(std::floor(aValue + 0.5f));\n53:   }\n54: \n55:   template <typename F>\n56:   static AuCoordImpl FromTruncate(F aValue) {\n57:     return AuCoordImpl(std::trunc(aValue));\n58:   }\n59: \n60:   template <typename F>\n61:   static AuCoordImpl FromCeil(F aValue) {\n62:     return AuCoordImpl(std::ceil(aValue));\n63:   }\n64: \n65:   template <typename F>\n66:   static AuCoordImpl FromFloor(F aValue) {\n67:     return AuCoordImpl(std::floor(aValue));\n68:   }\n69: \n70:   // Note: this returns the result of the operation, without modifying the\n71:   // original value.\n72:   [[nodiscard]] AuCoordImpl ToMinMaxClamped() const {\n73:     return std::clamp(this->value, kMin, kMax);\n74:   }\n75: \n76:   static constexpr Rep kMax = nscoord_MAX;\n77:   static constexpr Rep kMin = nscoord_MIN;\n78: };\n79: }  // namespace detail\n80: \n81: using AuCoord = detail::AuCoordImpl<int32_t>;\n82: using AuCoord64 = detail::AuCoordImpl<int64_t>;\n83: \n84: }  // namespace mozilla\n85: \n86: /**\n87:  * Divide aSpace by aN. and C++ layers.\\n\\n## Parsing (1): CSS calc() Expression Parsing\\n\\nThe primary parsing code lives in the Rust Servo style engine:\\n\\n- **`servo/components/style/values/specified/calc.rs`**: Defines the `MathFunction` enum (calc, min, max, clamp, round, mod, rem, sin, cos, tan, etc.), the `Leaf` enum for leaf node types (Length, Angle, Time, Resolution, Percentage, Number), and the `CalcLengthPercentage` struct. It also defines `AllowParse` which controls what units and additional functions (like anchor()) are permitted when parsing.\\n\\n- **`servo/components/style/values/generics/calc.rs`**: Defines `GenericCalcNode<L>` (the calc expression AST with Sum, Product, MinMax, Clamp, Round, ModRem, Negate, Invert, Hypot, Abs, Sign nodes) and implements `simplify_and_sort` (per CSS Values 4 simplification rules) and `resolve_internal` (the actual evaluation logic).\\n\\n## Overflow Handling (2): Numeric Overflow During calc() Resolution\\n\\nThe overflow/extreme-value handling happens at multiple layers:\\n\\n### Rust layer:\\n- **`servo/components/style/values/generics/calc.rs`** (`resolve_internal`): Handles IEEE 754 special values – NaN propagation for operations like round(infinity, infinity), and infinity passthrough for round(infinity, finite). The result after resolution is a float that may be infinity or NaN.\\n\\n- **`servo/components/style/values/computed/length_percentage.rs`** (`CalcLengthPercentage::resolve`): After resolving the calc tree, it applies `clamping_mode.clamp(px)` (using `AllowedNumericType` e.g. NonNegative) and `.normalized()` to handle the float result before returning a `Length`.\\n\\n### Rust-to-C++ bridge:\\n- **`servo/ports/geckolib/glue.rs`** (`Servo_ResolveCalcLengthPercentage`): Exported C-callable function that calls the Rust `resolve` and returns the result as a raw `f32`.\\n\\n### C++ layer:\\n- **`layout/style/ServoStyleConstsInlines.h`** (`StyleCalcLengthPercentage::Resolve`, `LengthPercentage::Resolve`, `detail::DefaultPercentLengthToAppUnits`): Converts the resolved CSS-pixel float to `nscoord` (int32_t app units) using `NSToCoordTruncClamped`. The `DefaultPercentLengthToAppUnits` function wraps the conversion with bounds-checking.\\n\\n- **`gfx/src/nsCoord.h`** (`NSToCoordRoundWithClamp`, `NSToCoordTruncClamped`): The core C++ overflow protection. Both functions explicitly bounds-check the float value against `nscoord_MAX` and `nscoord_MIN` before converting to `int32_t`, preventing overflow crashes when calc() produces extreme values like very large pixel lengths.\"\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":337,"cache_read_input_tokens":123373,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":337,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c6cc13aa-68e5-42df-8c37-7d3ef127ea71","uuid":"0853cf5a-40f1-4567-8746-f814dca1e543"}
